rationale: The citing paper discusses Du and Kamakura (2012) with a specific finding (common Google-search trends explaining a large share of U.S. auto sales) to support the legitimacy of search data in this market. It then positions its own contribution as an extension of prior Google Trends research by shifting from search-as-predictor work to a joint sales-and-search purchase-funnel model. This is more than a passing background mention because the cited study helps frame both the empirical motivation and the paper's claimed contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Du and Kamakura (2012) as prior evidence that online search dynamics are tightly linked to auto sales, and extends that line by modeling search as an intermediate funnel stage to decompose advertising effects into interest generation and conversion.
